title: Is it possible to hide a group?
excerpt:
Hi 
There is a group that I have of tasks yet to be scheduled on a board. Is it possible to have this group hidden from view so that only selected people can see it? 
Many Thanks 
Nick

posts.author: nickmaxwell (Nick Maxwell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi

There is a group that I have of tasks yet to be scheduled on a board. Is it possible to have this group hidden from view so that only selected people can see it?

Many Thanks

Nick

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nickmaxwell,

Right now, we don’t have the ability to hide groups from view, just columns using the advanced permissions.

If you don’t want to schedule them right now and don’t want them to be viewed yet by others, why not create a private backlog board (or main board) from which you can then move the tasks to your execution board when you’re ready for them to be worked on? That’s how we work here

posts.author: nickmaxwell (Nick Maxwell)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Julia - That sounds like a workable solution!

posts.author: Anja (Anja Dijkstra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

We would really love to be able to hide groups as well. The solution offered is fine if it is only one board, but we work with all our onboarding clients with taskboards and they get a bit daunting when they start. So it would be great if we can hide a group with tasks that are not due yet and add them to the list as we go, to keep the to-do list for our clients short & sweet.
would be great to have an automation for it too! ie if a status changes to done, dependency is moved to visible group or something nifty like that.
Thanks!
Anja

posts.author: Cande21 (Caitlin Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com how do you hide columns from view using the advance permissions?

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Cande21 See “How to restrict column view”

Board Permissions

posts.author: nerdgirlbritt (Brittany Stapleton)
posts.cookedHtml: I would really like to have this feature as well. Hiding a group on a specific board would allow for sharing a board and the client only seeing the tasks that they have without having a ton of completely separate boards. So essentially this would be group permissions you could also do this at the task level allowing for more customization and a better workflow @Julia-monday.com

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have you tried filtering the board to show just the groups you want to show to the client and then sharing the view via a shareable link with only the groups you’ve selected in the filter showing?

This article explains more!

posts.author: nerdgirlbritt (Brittany Stapleton)
posts.cookedHtml: This method does not work for us as we do not want to utilize the link option which gives tech-savvy users the ability to see everything. All of our workers are tech-savvy.

posts.author: rswisher (Russ Swisher)
posts.cookedHtml: I would particularly like this for the Kanban board. If I’m doing views for several teams when I filter the items out of the groups, the groups still show up blank. It makes it messy for software development in an agile environment.

posts.author: Julian (Julian Peters)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, any update on this topic. We also would need the function to hide groups or tasks within a shared board.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Julian and all, our developers are currently working on adding item level permissions, so that only certain people can view certain items. We are hoping to have this released by the end of the year

In terms of group level permissions, I would recommend adding this as a feature request here!: Submit an Idea - monday Community Forum

Feel free to let me know if you have any questions!

@rswisher, @nerdgirlbritt

posts.author: Julian (Julian Peters)
posts.cookedHtml: thanks a lot for the update. really looking forward to use this feature!

posts.author: ela (Ela)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TRB-monday.com , are there any updates on this?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, @ela - Yes! Item Permissions are available now on a limited by-approval basis for Enterprise plans only. I would be happy to provide a demo to you and chat with you about the feature if you are interested in learning more. You can book a time here: Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com

posts.cookedHtml:
I will like to see a short video on how this works. Can you upload this please?
Beside this, i would be happy to access this feature from our pro plan too

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @hlopezvc - Happy to, here’s a video walk through - monday.com Item Permissions...

Forgive the occasional chirping birds in the background, they are lively today in our neighborhood!

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: @PolishedGeek looks good… i hope Monday reconsider to release this function to pro accounts too. Do you know if there is something undergoing on the title of this thread? “Group Hiding”?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com (@Julia-monday.com @PolishedGeek @TRB-monday.com)

This feature is what we are looking for in order to setup our workflows to share our maintenance plan with our customers in 1 only board, with eficience (all in one board) and not having our customers, employees, collaborators to access to multiple boards.

IMO, this feature would be avaliable for most of all plans, because its a complementation of the item permission setup that exists in all accounts.

We are a low-medium company and we are ok with pro plan, so for 1 simple feature like that i cannot ask for the owners to pay a Enterprise plan.

Please i need this to be reviewed. And i think im not alone with this request…

There are many threads like this along this community posts.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
hlopezvc:

Do you know if there is something undergoing on the title of this thread? “Group Hiding”?

Not that I’m aware of @hlopezvc but I’ll keep my ears open and report back if I do!

posts.author: Gretchen (Gretchen Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
The item level permissions seems like a step in the right direction; however, it’s very disappointing this is only available on enterprise. We are a small team that works with clients. We’d like to be able to put all tasks related to a project on a board, and then simply have one group that’s internal-only tasks that only our team can see. Managing those on another board is really not scaleable because it duplicates the number of boards we have and makes it difficult to look at one project at a time in one place.

If we were to use Item level permissions, it seems to mean we would have to have two person columns – a single owner column and then another person column that has every team member on every item just so they can see task dependencies.

All in all, it’s disappointing that there are not permissions for groups. Is this on the roadmap for Pro plans?

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Gretchen @hlopezvc
We’re in the same position. As a nonprofit we simply can’t afford to move to an enterprise plan. Our inability to hide some groups or items from view on the Pro plan leads to very inefficient and non-sustainable workarounds. This feature really needs to be reviewed for inclusion in the Pro plan.
@Julia-monday.com @TRB-monday.com

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think this request Will be called as…

#Release_the_Item_permissions

posts.author: DarrenDNA (Darren)
posts.cookedHtml:
I completely agree with the community here. Monday is a phenomenal tool but to hide item view behind enterprise is insulting.

Consider this for a second.

You are business owner and you have resellers. You don’t want your resellers to steal the other contacts that other resellers have pulled into your system.

You also want the reseller to make changes on the board as they are the ones involved with the client.

You also don’t want each individual to have their own board because you want to have the ability to filter their progress on one board.

Does it not make sense to have a view that the reseller can only see items assigned to them? The items that they created?

That is a seriously basic feature that you would expect a system to have if you are promoting your system as a lead management system.

To hide this behind a massive pay gate is completely shameful and I am really disappointed that you guys have taken this approach. I seriously love your system but sometimes I feel that you just miss the basics.

Regards
Darren

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: Joshing (Joshua)
posts.cookedHtml: AGREED ! I currently have multiple boards for each one of my contractors and clients. My work around was using the automation to create and mirror on other board (main viewing board). However, I just found out that even that feature has limitations of only 20 linked boards to the mirroring board, so now I’m back to square one . Just can win .

posts.author: primamarca (Bruno)
posts.cookedHtml: I really need that… Im thinking in jump to another software just because of that.

posts.author: kylechlopcik (Kyle Chlopcik)
posts.cookedHtml: Any chance this will be implemented? I’m facing racing condition with my automations across several different boards. And to hide the group would be perfect workaround.

posts.author: jhamb (Josh Beck)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this question. The limitations of not being able to hide groups, and not being able to have groups on the same board with different columns are really hampering the usefulness of this tool for my company.

posts.author: AndreaMegawatt (Andrea)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature? I’m currently in need of it. Thanks.
